info.topictitle: B3.4.9 Wireless communication in embedded systems
info.parenttitle: B3.4 Electronic systems application and selection (HL only)

markdown:
## Embedded Systems


<definition term="Embedded System">A **computerised system** designed to **perform a specific set of tasks** within a larger device, often with **real-time control** and limited user interaction.</definition>

1. Embedded systems are often **resource-constrained**, meaning they have **limited processing power**, **memory**, and **energy**. This makes **efficient communication protocols **essential.
2. They are **integral** to **modern technology**, from **smartphones** to **industrial machinery**.

#### Why Communication Matters


1. **Interconnectivity**: Embedded systems often need to **exchange data** with other devices or systems.
2. **Functionality**: Communication enables **features** like **remote monitoring**, **control**, and **data sharing**.
3. **Efficiency**: Choosing the right protocol ensures **reliable** and **efficient** communication.

### Key Communication Protocols


### Wi-Fi


<definition term="Wi-Fi">A **wireless communication technology** that uses **radio waves** to provide **high-speed internet** and **network connectivity** without physical cables.</definition>

  1. **Range**: Medium to long (20–50m indoors, more outdoors)
  2. **Speed**: High (up to 1 Gbps with newer standards)
  3. **Power Usage**: High
  4. **Use Cases**: Streaming, file transfer, smart home devices, laptops
  5. **Pros**: Fast, widely supported, secure
  6. **Cons**: High power use, may interfere with other devices

<callout type="example">A **smart refrigerator **uses Wi-Fi to **send inventory data **to a **mobile app**, allowing users to **track food supplies **remotely.

![Smart Refrigerator (Asurion)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/563334103ba9342426e65b459605bc4a7a44412d-1024x576.png)</callout>

### Bluetooth


<definition term="Bluetooth">A **wireless communication standard** for **short-range data exchange** between **electronic devices**, such as phones, speakers, and computers.</definition>

  1. **Range**: Short (typically up to 10m)
  2. **Speed**: Moderate (up to 2 Mbps with Bluetooth 5)
  3. **Power Usage**: Low
  4. **Use Cases**: Wireless headphones, wearables, smart locks
  5. **Pros**: Low power, easy pairing
  6. **Cons**: Limited range and data rate

<callout type="analogy">Think of Bluetooth as a **walkie-talkie **for devices which are **short-ranged**, **low-powered**, and **designed **for **direct communication**.</callout>

![The Whoop bands sends data to the mobile app using Bluetooth (nextpit)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a0c97cb0d372902079eed9337d17b4ea9b2c7c40-1400x1400.png)

### 5G


<definition term="5G">The **fifth generation of mobile network technology**, offering **faster speeds**, **lower latency**, and **greater connectivity** than previous generations.</definition>

  1. **Range**: Wide (via cell towers)
  2. **Speed**: Very high (1–10 Gbps)
  3. **Power Usage**: Moderate to high
  4. **Use Cases**: Smart cities, autonomous vehicles, mobile IoT
  5. **Pros**: Ultra-fast, low latency, wide coverage
  6. **Cons**: Requires infrastructure, higher energy than Bluetooth

<callout type="note">While 5G offers **impressive capabilities**, it requires **significant infrastructure **and may not be **available **in all **areas**.</callout>

![The Tesla autonomous vehicles use 5G connectivity](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/eec481aded5d64a92caed3a7d0f8fa43fe9c3731-1200x675.png)

<callout type="note">* **Wi-Fi **is ideal for **high-speed**, **local networks**. 
* **Bluetooth **excels in **low-power**, **short-range **applications. 
* **5G **is best for **real-time**, **wide-area **communication.</callout>
